title: elachanthus pusillus
intro: Elachanthus pusillus, also known as the small white dodder, is a type of flowering plant in the broomrape family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Elachanthus pusillus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. Traditionally, it is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
